prompt:
The backup agent's snapshot planner uses our dirwalk package and customers keep asking for two things we can't do: skip junk (logs, node_modules, build output) and cap how deep we scan into vendor trees. Also the manifest differ chokes because plain lexical order interleaves files and directories — the UI team wants a tree-shaped listing.

What I need is a WalkFiltered(root string, opts Options) alongside the existing Walk (which must not change — the old manifest format depends on it byte for byte). Options carries:

- Excludes: gitignore-flavored patterns. A pattern without a slash matches the basename at any depth (*.log kills every log file anywhere; node_modules kills every node_modules). A pattern containing a slash is matched against the whole root-relative path, so docs/tmp only hits the one at the top, not other/docs/tmp. * never crosses a slash boundary. A trailing slash (tmp/) means directories only — a plain file named tmp survives it. When a directory is excluded, nothing under it is listed or even visited. A malformed pattern is an error, not a silent no-match.
- MaxDepth: 1 means just the root's children, 2 adds their children, and so on; zero or negative means unlimited (I want the zero value of Options to be a plain full walk).

Ordering must be deterministic and tree-shaped: depth-first, and inside every directory subdirectories come first (alphabetical), then files (alphabetical). Same Entry struct as Walk, same relative slash paths, sizes included.

filter_test.go is the acceptance suite (it builds everything in t.TempDir(), no fixtures to install); walk_test.go pins the old Walk. Both are read-only for this change. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... has to go green.
